name: Montana SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Montana's federal food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income households to purchase groceries. Administered by the Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Montana Medicaid
description: Montana's Medicaid program providing free or low-cost health coverage including doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, and behavioral health services for eligible residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL. Children: up to 261% FPL.

name: Montana Healthy Montana Kids (HMK Plus)
description: Montana's CHIP program providing health insurance coverage to children and teens in families that earn too much to qualify for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid but up to 261% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Up to 261% of the federal poverty level

name: Montana Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Montana Department of Labor and Industry.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: Montana Housing - Rental Assistance
description: Montana Housing provides affordable housing resources including rental assistance, Housing Choice Vouchers, and emergency housing programs for low-income Montanans.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income renters, families, seniors, and individuals experiencing housing instability
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 50-80% area median income

name: Montana Legal Services Association (MLSA)
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Montanans in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and Native American law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Montana residents at or below 200% FPL with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Montana Job Service
description: State workforce development offices providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, and connections to training programs. Locations across Montana.
eligibilitySummary: All Montana residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Montana LIEAP (Low Income Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income Montana households pay heating and energy bills during the winter season. Also provides weatherization and energy crisis assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 150% of federal poverty level or 60% of state median income
hours: Seasonal program, typically October through April

name: Montana 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Montanans to local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities assistance, childcare, and crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: All Montana residents seeking information about available community resources
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter services

name: Montana Food Bank Network
description: Statewide network of food banks and food pantries distributing food to Montanans facing hunger. Partners with Feeding America to supply food to over 350 local agencies across Montana.
eligibilitySummary: Any Montana resident experiencing food insecurity

name: Montana Veterans Affairs Division
description: Provides services and support to Montana veterans including benefits assistance, claims filing, education benefits, employment services, and connections to VA healthcare.
eligibilitySummary: Montana veterans and their eligible family members

name: Montana Crisis Hotline
description: Free, confidential crisis support for Montanans experiencing mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress. Available 24/7 with trained counselors.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Montana experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Montana Best Beginnings Child Care Scholarships
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work or attend school. Covers licensed childcare, group homes, and family childcare homes.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who need childcare to work or attend school
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Montana TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
description: Cash assistance program for low-income Montana families with children, providing monthly benefits along with employment and training services to help families achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children who meet work participation requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below state TANF limits, which vary by family size

name: Montana WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food, and referrals to health and social services for low-income pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Montana Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy upgrades to reduce heating and cooling costs for low-income households, including insulation, furnace repair or replacement, and air sealing.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority given to elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Montana Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment-related services to individuals with disabilities including career counseling, job training, assistive technology, and job placement to help them find and maintain employment.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Montana Senior and Long-Term Care
description: Programs for Montana seniors including Meals on Wheels, senior centers, in-home care services, respite care for caregivers, and the Senior Medicare Patrol program.
eligibilitySummary: Montana residents age 60 and older, and their caregivers

name: Disability Rights Montana
description: Montana's designated protection and advocacy system providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including abuse and neglect, access to services, and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: Montana residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing advocacy

name: Montana Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis support, safety planning, and referrals to local domestic violence shelters and services for survivors of domestic and intimate partner violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or stalking in Montana
languages: English, Spanish, and 200+ languages via interpreter services

name: Montana Public Transit
description: The Montana Department of Transportation supports public transit services across the state, including rural and urban bus services, paratransit, and transportation for seniors and people with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: All Montana residents; specialized services for seniors, people with disabilities, and rural communities

name: Montana Supplemental Security Income (SSI) Assistance
description: The Social Security Administration provides SSI benefits to Montana residents who are aged, blind, or disabled with limited income and resources. State DPHHS can help with applications.
eligibilitySummary: Aged (65+), blind, or disabled individuals with limited income and resources

name: Montana Board of Housing - Homebuyer Programs
description: Offers affordable mortgage programs, down payment assistance, and homebuyer education for first-time and low-income homebuyers in Montana.
eligibilitySummary: First-time homebuyers and low-to-moderate income Montana residents
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by county and household size

name: Head Start / Early Head Start Montana
description: Federally funded early childhood education program providing comprehensive services including education, health, nutrition, and family support for low-income children from birth to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children from birth to age 5, pregnant women, and children in foster care
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level; categorical eligibility for foster children, homeless families, and TANF/SSI recipients

name: Montana Office of Public Instruction - Adult Education
description: Provides adult basic education, GED preparation, English as a Second Language classes, and high school equivalency programs for Montana adults seeking to improve their education.
eligibilitySummary: Montana adults age 16 and older who are not enrolled in secondary school

name: Billings Food Bank
description: Serves Yellowstone County and surrounding areas with emergency food distribution, mobile food pantries, and the BackPack Program for school children facing weekend hunger.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of Yellowstone County and surrounding areas experiencing food insecurity

name: City of Billings Community Development
description: Administers federal housing and community development programs in Billings including CDBG, HOME, and Emergency Solutions Grants for housing rehabilitation, homelessness prevention, and neighborhood improvement.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income residents of Billings
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 80% area median income for most programs

name: Missoula Food Bank & Community Center
description: Provides free groceries, hot meals, and community resources to Missoula County residents in need. Also offers cooking classes and nutrition education.
eligibilitySummary: Missoula County residents experiencing food insecurity

name: Great Falls Community Food Bank
description: Distributes food to families and individuals in need throughout north central Montana. Operates a warehouse and supports dozens of partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in north central Montana facing food insecurity

name: Mountain Line (Missoula Urban Transportation District)
description: Zero-fare public transit service in Missoula providing bus routes throughout the city. All rides are completely free to all riders.
eligibilitySummary: All riders; completely free public transit in Missoula

name: Montana Indian Country SNAP Outreach
description: Specialized SNAP outreach and enrollment assistance for tribal communities across Montana's seven reservations, helping Native Americans access food assistance benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Native American residents on or near Montana reservations seeking SNAP benefits
incomeLimitNotes: Standard SNAP income limits apply; some tribal members may qualify for expanded deductions

name: Montana 2-1-1 Online Resource Directory
description: Searchable online database of social services in Montana, maintained by the Montana 211 system. Find food, housing, healthcare, and other resources by location and category.
eligibilitySummary: All Montana residents seeking community resource information
